description: Cost-optimization epic, candidate #7 (owner-approved 2026-07-08). The 'digest contract' bet: keep Sonnet ONLY for the editorial selection decision -- the one phase where intelligence has measurably mattered (content 4 has only ever come with Sonnet in the selection seat) -- but shrink what Sonnet reads. multiagent-aggressive-haiku's Sonnet selection thread cost $1.21 because it read the research phase's FULL raw context (1.13M cache-read tokens); here the Haiku research sub-agent must emit a COMPACT STRUCTURED DIGEST (/workspace/research-digest.json: per candidate story a 2-3 sentence summary, source links, and a prior-overlap flag -- never raw dumps), and the Sonnet selection sub-agent reads ONLY that digest plus the (small) prior briefs, cutting its context roughly 5-10x (projected selection cost ~$0.15-0.25, candidate total ~$0.80-1.00 vs baseline $1.91 with content-4 quality). The Haiku writing sub-agent then writes the brief from selection.json + the digest, RE-FETCHING each selected story's source links for accurate detail (fetching ~8 selected stories on Haiku is cheap; every phase context stays small). Two hard lessons from the 2026-07-07 haiku-swap-verified failure are designed in: (1) the HAIKU coordinator gets STRUCTURAL synchronization, not instructions to be patient -- after each delegation it must run a mechanical file-existence gate (`until [ -f <phase output> ]; do sleep 15; done`) before proceeding, converting 'wait' into a step Haiku demonstrably follows; (2) the coordinator is explicitly forbidden from writing any content file itself -- its only jobs are delegate, gate, and cat. Dedup diligence lives in TWO explicit places: the research sub-agent must read every prior brief (mandatory cat step) and mark prior-overlap per digest entry; the selection sub-agent must require follow-up labels in selection.json for overlapping picks, which the writer applies. Content generation ONLY: holds no AWS/delivery role (FR-1), never fans out, never synthesizes audio; only delivery-side contact is the read-only GET /recent-briefs route. Coordinator and all four sub-agents are SEPARATE, newly-created agent resources.
schedule_intent: none -- cost-optimization eval candidate only; never scheduled and never promoted to production without a separate, explicitly-approved cut-over decision
